intro: Languas romblonensis, also known as the Romblon lenga lenga, is a plant species that belongs to the Zingiberaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of ginger. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Languas romblonensis include gingerols, shogaols, and other volatile compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used in the form of tea or capsules to utilize its health benefits.
